questions.body: A 60 kg weight is dragged on a horizontal surface by a rope upto 2 metres. If coefficient of friction is μ = 0.5, the angle of rope with the surface is 60° and g = 9.8 m/sec², then work done is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Let body is dragged with force P, making an angle 60° with the horizontal. f_k = Kinetic friction in the motion = μ_k R. From the figure f_k = P cos60° and R = mg - P sin60°. ∴ P cos60° = μ_k (mg - P sin60°). ⇒ P/2 = 0.5(60 × 10 - P√3/2) ⇒ P = 315.1 N. ∴ f_k = P cos60° = 315.1/2 N. Work done = f_k × s = 315.1/2 × 2 = 315 Joule

questions.body: The total energy of the body executing S.H.M. is E. Then the kinetic energy when the displacement is half of the amplitude, is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Total energy in SHM E = 1/2 mω²a² ; (where a = amplitude). Kinetic energy U = 1/2 mω²(a² - y²) = E - 1/2 mω²y². When y = a/2 ⇒ U = E - 1/2 mω²(a²/4) = E - E/4 = 3E/4

questions.body: In the adjoining diagram, a wavefront AB, moving in air is incident on a plane glass surface XY. Its position CD after refraction through a glass slab is shown also along with the normals drawn at A and D. The refractive index of glass with respect to air (μ = 1) will be equal to
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
In the case of refraction if CD is the refracted wave front and v₁ and v₂ are the speed of light in the two media, then in the time the wavelets from B reaches C, the wavelet from A will reach D, such that
t = BC/v_w = AD/v_g ⇒ BC/AD = v_w/v_g .....(i)
But in ΔACB, BC = AC sinθ .....(ii)
while in ΔACD, AD = AC sinθ' .....(iii)
From equations (i), (ii) and (iii) v_w/v_g = sinθ/sinθ'
Also μ ∝ 1/v ⇒ v_w/v_g = μ_g/μ_w = sinθ/sinθ'
⇒ μ_g = sinθ/sinθ'

questions.body: Consider the circuits shown in the figure. Both the circuits are taking same current from battery but current through R in the second circuit is 1/10 th of current through R in the first circuit. If R is 11 Ω, the value of R₁
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
In figure (b) current through
R₃ = i - i/10 = 9i/10
Potential difference across R₃ = Potential difference across
R ⇒ R₃ × 9i/10 = R × i/10
i.e. R₃ = R/9 = 11/9 Ω
R_eq = (R₃ × R)/(R₃ + R) = (11/9 × 11)/(11/9 + 11) = 11/10 Ω
Total circuit resistance = 11/10 + R₁ = R = 11 ⇒ R₁ = 9.9Ω

questions.subject: physics

Where, h = depth of lake
⇒ hρg = 7P₀ ⇒ h = 7 × (Hρg/ρg) = 7H₀

questions.body: The quadratic equation x² + mx + n = 0 has roots that are twice those of x² + px + m = 0, and none of m, n and p is zero. What is the value of n/p ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let the roots of x² + px + m = 0 be r₁ and r₂. The sum of the roots is r₁ + r₂ = −p. The product is r₁r₂ = m. The roots of x² + mx + n = 0 are 2r₁ and 2r₂. The product of these roots is 4r₁r₂ = n. The sum of these roots is −m = 2(r₁ + r₂) = −2p ⟺ m = 2p.

We need n/p = 4r₁r₂/p = 4m/p = 4(2p)/p = 8

questions.body: A triangle is partitioned into three triangles and a quadrilateral by drawing two lines from vertices to their opposite sides. The areas of the three triangles are 3, 7, and 7, as shown. What is the area of the shaded quadrilateral?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
If C is the cevian intersection with AC, E the intersection of the other cevian with AB and {F} = BD ∩ CE, see that BF = FD and EF/FC = 3/7

Applying Menelaus to △ABD with the transversal CFE we get BE/AE = CD/AC, while Menelaus from △ACE with transversal BDF: CD/AD = 7·BE/3·AB, multiplying these 2 relations side by side,

one gets: AE·AD/AB·AC = 3/7

This last relation means S_ADE/S_ABC = 3/7. Seeing that S_DEF = 3, from the above relation we get S_ADE = 15; adding S_DEF = 3, finally S_AEFD = 18

questions.body: Circles of diameter 1 inch and 3 inches have the same center. The smaller circle is painted red, and the portion outside the smaller circle and inside the larger circle is painted blue. What is the ratio of the blue-painted area to the red-painted area?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: The smaller circle has area pi. The large circle has area 9pi. The blue area has area 9pi-pi=8pi. The ratio is 8pi/pi=8. A.

questions.body: In a triangle with integer side lengths, one side is three times as long as a second side, and the length of the third side is 15. What is the greatest possible perimeter of the triangle?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: By triangle inequality, 15 has to have the middle length. The largest value of x which satisfies x + 15 > 3x is x = 7, so we have 7 + 15 + 21 = 43 ⇒ (A)

questions.body: Which of the following is equivalent to x/(x-1) * sqrt(1-x/x) when x < 0?

questions.body: Let a1, a2,... be a sequence for which a1 = 2, a2 = 3 and an = an-1/an-2 for each positive integer n ≥ 3. What is a2019?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: we see that the first few terms are 2,3,3/2, 1/2, 1/3, 2/3, 2,3, and so on. It repeats with period 6. 2006 is 2 greater than a multiple of 6, so the answer is 3. (D)

questions.body: In rectangle ABCD, we have A = (6, -22), B = (2006, 178) and D = (8, y), for some integer y. What is the area of rectangle ABCD?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
AB ⊥ AD
So, the slope of segment AB is equal to the negative reciprocal of the slope of segment AD.
(yb - ya)/(xb - xa) = -1/(yd - ya)/(xd - xa)
(178 + 22)/(2006 - 6) = -(8 - 6)/(y + 22)
200/2000 = -2/(y + 22)
1/10 = -2/(y + 22)
-1 = 20/(y + 22)
-y - 22 = 20
-y = 42
y = -42
Because ABCD is a rectangle, A_ABCD = (AB)(AD)
AB = sqrt((2006 - 6)^2 + (178 + 22)^2)
AB = sqrt((2000)^2 + (200)^2)
AB = sqrt(4000000 + 400000)
AB = sqrt(4400000)
AB = 100sqrt(404)

AD = sqrt((8 - 6)^2 + (-42 + 22)^2)
AD = sqrt(2^2 + (-20)^2)
AD = sqrt(4 + 400)
AD = sqrt(404)

A_ABCD = (AB)(AD) = 100sqrt(404)sqrt(404) = 40400 = (D)

questions.body: Which one of the following quantities is irrational?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Option (A) (1 - tan^2 30)/(1 + tan^2 30) = (1 - (1/sqrt(3))^2)/(1 + (1/sqrt(3))^2) = (1 - 1/3)/(1 + 1/3) = (2/3)/(4/3) = 1/2 (rational)

option (B) 4 cos^4 30 - 3 cos 30 = cos 3 × 30 = cos 90 = 0 (rational)
option (C) 3 sin 30 - 4 sin^3 30 = sin 3 × 30 = sin 90 = 1 (rational)
option (D) (2cot30°)/(cot^2 30-1) = (2(sqrt(3)))/(sqrt(3))^2-1 = (2sqrt(3))/2 = sqrt(3) (irrational)

8! = 40320

From 10! and on, we see that the ten's digit is simply 0. Therefore, we add 4 + 2 + 8 = 4 → C

questions.body: A rectangle has area A cm^2 and perimeter P cm, where A and P are positive integers. Which of the following numbers cannot equal A + P ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
A = lw
P = 2l + 2w
A + P = lw + 2l + 2w
A + P + 4 = (l + 2)(w + 2)
We are given that l and w are positive integers (see the proof above); obviously the only numbers that cannot be made as factors are 1 and 2.
Thus we are looking for a number such that n + 4 is the product of 2 and a prime.
Checking, we see n = 102

questions.body: How many pairs of positive integers (a, b) are there such that gcd (a, b) = 1 and a/b + 14b/9a is an integer?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let a/b + 14b/9a = n for some integer n. 9a² + 14b² = 9abn9 has to divide b². Otherwise, since 9 clearly divides 9a² and 9abm, the left hand side would not be divisible by n and the right hand side would. For 9 to divide b², either 3 or 9 divides b. If 9 divides it, then the right hand side would be divisible by 81 but the left hand side would still only be divisible by 9. That means only 3 can divide b. If any other number divides it, it would also divide the right hand side but not the left hand side, because a and b are relatively prime. Thus the only possible value of b is 3.
If something divides a except for 1, 2, 7, and 14 then it would not divide the term 14b², but it would divide the right hand side and the term 9a² and so this is impossible. So the only possible values of a are 1, 2, 7, 14
We then have four possibilities of (a, b): (1, 3), (2, 3), (7, 3), and (14, 3). If we plug them in we see that they all work, so the answer is (A)

questions.body: There are two values of a for which the equation 4x^2 + ax + 8x + 9 = 0 has only one solution for x. What is the sum of these values of a?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
4x² + (8 + a)x + 9 = 0 must be a perfect square.
so we have (2x + 3)² = 0 or (2x - 3)² = 0,
which give b = 8 + a = ±12 so we have a = 4, -20, and the sum of the values is -16 → A

questions.body: Which of the following numbers is a perfect square?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Notice how (17!!8!)/(2) = ((17!)^2 · 18)/(2) = 17!^2 · 9. This is obviously a perfect square, so our answer is (B)

questions.body: Suppose that m and n are positive integers such that 75m = n^3. What is the minimum possible value of m + n?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: 75 factorizes as 5^2 × 3. If 75m = n^3, so the exponents of each of the prime factors of 75m must be multiples of 3. Then 75m = 3^3 × 5^3 ⟹ m = 3^2 × 5 and n = ∛(75m) = 3^(1/3) × 5^(1/3) = 3 × 5 = 15 and so m + n = 45 + 15 = 60. Alternate: We have m + n = n^3/75 + n. Since 5|n, write n = 5k. Then m = (125k^3)/(75) = (5k^3)/(3), so put k = 3. Then m = 45 and n = 15, so m + n ≥ 60. (C)

questions.body: Rectangle ABCD has AB = 8 and BC = 6.. Point M is the midpoint of diagonal AC, and E is on AB with ME ⊥ AC. What is the area of △AME ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: It can be shown that △AME ~ △ABC, because they both have a right angle and they share an angle in common. Thus, the scale factor of △AME to △ABC is 5/8. Therefore, ME = 6 · (5/8) = 15/4, and AM = 5, so the area is AM · ME = (1/2) · 5 · (15/4) = 75/8 (D)

questions.body: Suppose that a cows give b gallons of milk in c days. At this rate, how many gallons of milk will d cows give in e days?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Note that we need to multiply b by d/a for the new supply of cows and c/c for the extra time, so (A)

questions.body: There are 24 four-digit whole numbers that use each of the four digits 2, 4, 5 and 7 exactly once. Only one of these four-digit numbers is a multiple of another one. Which of the following is it?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: The number must be either 2 or 3 times the smaller. We can easily check each option to find that 7425 = 3 · 2475 and the answer is (C).
